title: Schema Registry & Kafka Connect
learningObjectives: Explain **Schema Registry** roles, **subject** strategies, and **compatibility** modes
Operate **Kafka Connect** with tasks, converters, transforms, and DLQ discipline
Prevent **breaking** schema changes using **CI** and governance processes
Design **idempotent** sinks and **keying** strategies for **at-least-once** delivery
Secure registry and connector credentials with rotation and least privilege

sections.title: Why Schema Registry & Kafka Connect matters
sections.content:
**Schema Registry** and **Connect** operationalize **contracts** at **scale**. Strong candidates articulate **compatibility** modes, **connector** failure surfaces, and **DLQ** hygiene—not just buzzwords.

Financial and compliance orgs care about **PII** in schemas and **sink** idempotency.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Schema Registry & Kafka Connect
sections.content:
### Day 64 — **Schema** **Registry** + **Kafka** **Connect**

### 1. Registry purpose
Versioned **schemas** + **REST** + **compatibility** policies.

### 2. Compatibility
**Backward**, **forward**, **full**, **transitive** nuances.

### 3. Connect
Source/sink **tasks**, **converters**, **transforms**.

### 4. Failure handling
**errors.tolerance**, **DLQ** topics, **restarts**.

### 5. Data quality
**CI** checks for **breaking** changes.

### 6. Story
**Auto-register** in prod causes incompatible **Avro** → CI gate + **rollback**.

sections.title: Basic — Registry + Connect vocabulary
sections.output:
registry: central schema versions + compatibility rules
connect: source/sink integration framework
subject: named schema lineage used by serdes

sections.title: Intermediate — Compatibility mode labels
sections.output:
backward: new schema reads old data
forward: old schema reads new data

sections.title: Advanced — Subject name builder toy
Connect sink with converters
sections.description: Tasks read Kafka, write to DB with schema-aware deserialization.
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 64 — Connect sink
queue Kafka
participant ConnectorTask
database Database
Kafka -> ConnectorTask : poll records
ConnectorTask -> Database : upsert with pk.mode
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Schema Registry & Connect — task restart causes (Day 64 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly**: DB auth failure, schema mismatch, or deserialization error
hints: Name one **metric** and one **config** you would check on-call for this day’s topic.
State a **failure mode** (e.g. rebalance, ISR shrink, schema break) tied to your answer.

sections.title: Use cases — Schema Registry & Kafka Connect
sections.content:
## Use cases — **Schema Registry & Kafka Connect**

### 1) **Contract** **CI** for **events**
**Context:** **Hundreds** of **schemas** owned by **services**; **breaking** changes **slip** **out**.
**Why Day 64:** **Compatibility** **mode** + **subject** **strategy** is **governance**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **CI** **check** **BACKWARD** for **consumers** still on **old** **code**.
2. **Subject** naming per **topic** **strategy** (**TopicNameStrategy** vs **RecordNameStrategy**).
3. **Pin** **schema** **id** in **incident** **runbooks** for **replay**.
**Failure hook:** **Deserializer** **error** **poisons** **partition** after **schema** **push**.

### 2) **Postgres** → **Kafka** **CDC** **connector**
**Context:** **Operational** **store** **feeds** **analytics** **lake**.
**Why Day 64:** **Tasks**, **offsets**, and **SMT** **pipelines** need **ops** **ownership**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **SingleMessageTransform** for **PII** **masking** — **review** in **PR**.
2. **errors.tolerance** + **DLQ** **topic** for **bad** **rows**.
3. **Restart** **policy** with **alert** on **task** **FAILED**.
**Failure hook:** **Silent** **drop** from **over-aggressive** **SMT**.

### 3) **Snowflake** **sink** **connector**
**Context:** **Warehouse** **load** **must** **keep** **up** with **peak** **Kafka** **throughput**.
**Why Day 64:** **Tasks** **scale** with **partitions** and **worker** **count**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Match **tasks.max** to **throughput** and **Snowflake** **ingest** **limits**.
2. Monitor **consumer** **lag** on **connector** **internal** **topics**.
**Failure hook:** **Schema** **evolution** **breaks** **flatten** **SMT** **mapping**.

### 4) **Partner** **integration** **with** **Avro**
**Context:** **External** **team** **consumes** **your** **topics**.
**Why Day 64:** **Forward** compatibility for **their** **release** **cadence**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Document **compatibility** **mode** and **allowed** **evolution** **patterns**.
2. Provide **example** **records** + **schema** **diff** in **release** **notes**.
**Failure hook:** **Field** **removal** **without** **default** **breaks** **older** **readers**.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is **Schema Registry**?
conceptual.answer: Central **schema** **store** with **compatibility** rules and **REST** access for **producer**/**consumer** serdes. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is **Schema Registry**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **BACKWARD** compatibility meaning?
conceptual.answer: New **schema** can read **data** written with **previous** schema (common default). Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****BACKWARD** compatibility meaning?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **FORWARD** vs **FULL**?
conceptual.answer: **Forward** allows old code to read new data; **full** is intersection of **forward** + **backward**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****FORWARD** vs **FULL**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What is **Kafka Connect**?
conceptual.answer: Framework for **source** and **sink** **connectors** moving data between Kafka and systems like **JDBC**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

conceptual.question: **SMT** (Single Message Transforms)?
conceptual.answer: Lightweight **map** operations in connector pipeline (mask fields, route topics). For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

conceptual.question: **DLQ** in Connect?
conceptual.answer: Route **failed** records to a **dead-letter** topic with **error** metadata. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

conceptual.question: What does **consumer lag** tell you that **broker CPU** does not?
conceptual.answer: **Lag** ties **processing** speed and **fetch** cadence to **business** backlog per partition. **CPU** can look healthy while **lag** explodes due to **slow handlers**, **poison** messages, or **downstream** calls—**SLO** on lag is closer to user impact.

conceptual.answer: A tombstone with a key signals **delete** in compaction semantics after cleanup policies run. Consumers and **stream** apps must handle **null** values and **ordering** with care or **state stores** become wrong.

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// curl -s http://registry/subjects/orders-value/versions/latest** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

codeBased.question: What is the purpose of **`linger.ms`** and **`batch.size`** together?
codeBased.answer: **Linger** waits to **fill** batches for **higher** compression and **throughput**; **batch.size** caps bytes. **Tuning** trades **latency** vs **efficiency**—watch **p99** produce latency when increasing linger.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **deserialize** errors spike after deploy.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Pause connector; inspect **schemas**.

**(2) Root cause** **Incompatible** evolution reached prod.

**(3) Fix** Rollback producer; fix **CI** gate.

**(4) Prevention** Dashboard registry **409** conflicts. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Incident:** **deserialize** errors spike after deploy.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Ops:** **Sink** duplicates rows hourly.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Check **keys**.

**(2) Root cause** At-least-once without primary key.

**(3) Fix** Add **PK** + **upsert** mode.

**(4) Prevention** Reconcile job. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

seniorScenario.answer:

**(2) Root cause** Couples evolution unnecessarily.

**(3) Fix** Split subjects per type.

**(4) Prevention** ADR. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. wrongAnswers: Registry makes topics **ordered** — **Correction:** Ordering still partition semantics.
**Backward** means old writers OK with new readers always — **Correction:** Direction matters; define clearly.
Connect replaces **Streamer** apps — **Correction:** Different fit for **bulk** integration.
**JSON** without registry is safer — **Correction:** Still need contracts and evolution discipline.
**tasks.max** infinite helps — **Correction:** Bounded by partitions and DB load.
**DLQ** optional always — **Correction:** Needed for poison resilience.
**Schema** evolution is only registry concern — **Correction:** Consumers must deploy with compatible code.

sections.title: MCQ Quiz — Schema Registry & Kafka Connect
sections.description: Thirty questions from basic to advanced — Schema Registry & Kafka Connect. Read every option; distractors are plausible but wrong for a precise reason.

questions.question: [Schema Registry & Connect] Which statement best describes **Avro schema** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **Avro schema** belongs to the operational model for **Schema Registry & Connect**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Schema Registry & Connect] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **BACKWARD compatibility**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **BACKWARD compatibility** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Schema Registry & Connect] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **FORWARD compatibility**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **FORWARD compatibility** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **FULL compatibility** belongs to the operational model for **Schema Registry & Connect**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Schema Registry & Connect] You see misconfiguration around **subject naming** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **subject naming** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.explanation: **BACKWARD compatibility** belongs to the operational model for **Schema Registry & Connect**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Schema Registry & Connect] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **FORWARD compatibility**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **FORWARD compatibility** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Schema Registry & Connect] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **FULL compatibility**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **FULL compatibility** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Schema Registry & Connect] Which statement best describes **subject naming** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **subject naming** belongs to the operational model for **Schema Registry & Connect**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Schema Registry & Connect] You see misconfiguration around **Schema Registry** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **Schema Registry** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Schema Registry & Connect] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **connector task**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **connector task** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Schema Registry & Connect] Which statement best describes **SMT** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **SMT** belongs to the operational model for **Schema Registry & Connect**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Schema Registry & Connect] You see misconfiguration around **dead letter queue** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **dead letter queue** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **converter** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

sections.title: Schema Registry & Kafka Connect — cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Rule of thumb | Interview one-liner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Item | Recall | Recall |
| Registry | Versioned schemas | Versioned schemas |
| BACKWARD | New read old default | New read old default |
| Subject | orders-value naming | orders-value naming |
| Connect task | Partition-bound work | Partition-bound work |
| DLQ | Poison handling | Poison handling |
| SMT | Field level transforms | Field level transforms |
| Upsert | Needs PK | Needs PK |
| CI compat | Blocks prod breaks | Blocks prod breaks |
| offsets.topic | Internal connect progress | Internal connect progress |
| Debezium | CDC to Kafka | CDC to Kafka |
| Operational metric | Watch lag + ISR + request latency | Say lag per partition and under-replicated partitions |
| Failure drill | Broker bounce + consumer crash chaos | Prove rebalance and recovery SLOs in staging |
| Capacity | Partitions bound parallelism per group | More consumers than partitions stay idle |
| Security | ACLs least privilege per principal | Separate prod cluster or strict topic governance |
